titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 734

p.seg: To all who shall view or hear these letters, Patrick, son of Patrick earl of Dunbar, sends greeting.

Know me faithfully to have understood and recognised through the charter of King Edgar, and by many other proofs, in the court of the lord king, Alexander, the full right of the prior and monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham in respect of the toun and demesne of Swinewood with its pertinents concerning which I have at some time disturbed them. Hence it is that I make known to you all that I, for myself and my heirs, have quitclaimed and renounced for ever, to God, Saint Cuthbert, Saint Ebbe, and the prior and monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham, and who will in perpetuity serve there, the toun of Swinewood and the entire demesne of the same toun, with all its pertinents, and the whole right and every kind of right and claim, if I had or could have had it in the same toun, as well in demesnes as in men, services and homages, woods and plains, lands cultivated and uncultivated, and all other things belonging to the said toun and the said demesne, without any withholding; and I returned by rod and staff in the king's court what my ancestors unawfully appropriated and withheld, so that neither I nor my heirs nor any on our side could have in the future right or claim in the aforementioned toun and demesne with its pertinents.

The said quitclaim was made in the court of the lord king, Alexander, illustrious king of the Scots, at Roxburgh on the third kalends of April [30 March] in the seventeenth year of reign of the same king.
So that everything aforesaid should remain valid and undisputed, I have authenticated the present writing by the application of my seal.
